chapter: D. NEURONAL MIGRATION ANOMALY due to infection, ischemia, metabolic disorders

differentials.name: Hemangioma (a)  capillary hemangioma: seen in children, involution by 7 years of age in 95% (b)  cavernous hemangioma: seen in adults, no involution
differentials.raw: Hemangioma (a)  capillary hemangioma: seen in children, involution by 7 years of age in 95% (b)  cavernous hemangioma: seen in adults, no involution

differentials.name: Brain ischemia ← steal phenomenon by AV fistula
differentials.raw: Brain ischemia ← steal phenomenon by AV fistula

context: Blunt Cerebrovascular Injury =  carotid (CA) + vertebral artery (VA) injuries during generalized multitrauma / direct craniocervical trauma Prevalence:   1.1-1.6% of all blunt trauma Mechanism:   partial / complete failure of arterial mural integrity ← longitudinal stretching of artery, direct blow to artery, piercing by bone fragment Prognosis:   25-38% mortality if injury untreated Cx:   (1)   Infarction ← intimal disruption / flap / hematoma →  thromboembolism of platelet aggregates →  critical luminal stenosis + occlusion
